title: boechera harrisonii
intro: Boechera harrisonii, also known as Harrison's rockcress, is a perennial wildflower plant native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and supporting respiratory health. The herbal preparations of Boechera harrisonii include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
